hero.sub: Open-source platform to actually explore Spanish legislation. A knowledge graph wires every cross-reference together, an AI chat cites the real BOE, and dashboards show how the law evolves over time.

stats.label: Integrated layers sharing data — API, graph, chat & dashboards
LLM providers supported through MCP tools
Spanish laws indexed from the legalize-es corpus
Open source under the Apache 2.0 license

whatIs.title: LexFlow in two paragraphs.
whatIs.anchor: LexFlow is an open-source platform (Apache 2.0) that turns the full body of Spanish legislation from the BOE — around 12,000 laws in Markdown from the legalize-es project — into a navigable product with four layers: full-text search, a cross-reference graph, an AI chat that cites the real BOE, and legislative analytics dashboards, all running locally with no cloud dependencies.
whatIs.p1: LexFlow is an **open-source platform** that takes the full body of Spanish legislation — the BOE corpus, already in Markdown thanks to the **legalize-es** project — and turns it into something walkable: fast search, a graph of cross-references between statutes, a chat that cites the exact article, and dashboards that show how the law shifts over time.
whatIs.p2: The goal is simple: anyone who works with the law — or just wants to understand it — should stop losing hours jumping between BOE tabs. Everything runs locally, no cloud round-trips, and the code is public so anyone can review or improve it.

devContrib.title: Built in the open, made to be contributed to.
devContrib.body: LexFlow is a public, free project under the Apache 2.0 license. The vision is to build it together with everyone who cares about Spanish legislation being easier to understand. If you can bring something to the table — code, ideas, copy fixes, translations, accessibility tweaks — it all counts.
devContrib.bullet2: Public roadmap — no surprises about what's coming
devContrib.bullet3: No account, no SaaS — everything runs on your machine

personas.title: Made for people who live with the law every day.
personas.sub: You don't need to know how to code. LexFlow is built for the people who already fight the BOE every day and need a less tedious way to do it.

list.pain: Cross-reference statutes without opening twenty BOE tabs and without losing track of which amends what.
Ask the syllabus instead of digging through it. Every answer comes with the exact article cited.
Trace a law from its publication through every amendment, and see which other laws cite it.
Sector-scoped alerts when a regulation changes, instead of watching the BOE by hand every morning.

layersTitle: What LexFlow takes off your plate.
layersSub: Four concrete problems that show up when you work with Spanish legislation — and how the product solves each one. The tech detail sits in **For devs** below, if you care how it's built.

layers.title: Find any law without opening the BOE.
layers.body: Full catalogue of Spanish legislation with instant full-text search, filters by rank, scope and status, and the full text of every article one click away.
bullets: Full-text search across the whole corpus, not just titles
Filter by rank, scope and validity status
Full article text, with BOE metadata attached
Fast pagination — no spinner even on thousands of results

layers.title: See the network of statutes, not a flat list.
layers.body: Every cross-reference becomes a walkable edge. Browse neighbours, find the shortest path between two laws and surface the corpus's most central pieces.
bullets: Neighbours of any law or article in one click
Most central pieces of the corpus surfaced by PageRank
Filter by topic, period or relation type

layers.title: Ask the BOE like a teammate.
layers.body: Bring your own model and the chat answers with text quoted from the real corpus — not from what the model remembers from training. With Ollama it runs offline.
bullets: Five AI providers behind a single selector
Every answer comes with the exact article cited
Local-first — with Ollama everything stays on your machine

layers.title: Watch how the law evolves over time.
layers.body: Interactive dashboards showing reform pace, the most-amended statutes and sector-scoped alerts whenever something shifts.
bullets: Reform pace by year and by sector
Top statutes by number of amendments
CSV export of any filtered slice

userFlow.title: Three steps. No Python, no Docker.
userFlow.sub: LexFlow downloads, opens and that's it. The user flow is the same across every operating system.

steps.title: Open LexFlow
steps.body: One executable per OS. No Python install, no dependencies, no Docker. Just download and open.
steps.bullet: Available on Windows, macOS and Linux

steps.title: Search or browse
steps.body: Type the law's name or its BOE number. If you'd rather explore, the graph shows you everything around a given statute.

steps.title: Ask or export
steps.body: The chat answers with the exact article cited. Dashboards give you the big picture. Any result exports to CSV or JSON.

howItWorks.eyebrow: How it works under the hood
howItWorks.title: From Markdown to answer, in one pass.
howItWorks.sub: Each layer rests on the one below it. The parser feeds the graph, the graph grounds the chat, and the API is the single source of truth for every client.

scenes.title: Parser and registry
scenes.body: Markdown from legalize-es, YAML frontmatter, recursive sections. The output is a typed LawRegistry the rest of the stack queries — no reparsing later.

scenes.title: Knowledge graph
scenes.body: Cross-references resolve to BOE identifiers and land as graph edges. PageRank, communities and shortest paths all live on the same NetworkX graph.

scenes.title: Chat with MCP tools
scenes.body: The chosen model (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) calls search_law, get_neighbors or get_article — and the chat answers with real citations from the corpus.

builtOnTitle: Powered by legalize-es. Shipped as a single binary.
builtOnBody: LexFlow is a thin, intelligent layer over legalize-es — a public Git-versioned repository of Spanish legislation in Markdown. The final deliverable is a standalone binary per OS. No Docker, no Python install — download and run.

builtOnTerminal.text: # Clone, sync the corpus, run the server

stackSub: No experimental frameworks, no proprietary cloud. Every piece is open source, well-documented and easy to swap out without rewriting the rest.
roadmapTitle: A public roadmap, phase by phase.
roadmapSub: LexFlow is being built in the open. The backend is feature-complete; the next milestones live in the React frontend.

roadmap.name: Foundation
roadmap.desc: Repo scaffolding, CI/CD, branch protection, dependabot.

roadmap.name: Core API
roadmap.desc: legalize-es parser, REST endpoints, full-text search.

roadmap.name: Knowledge graph
roadmap.desc: NetworkX model, PageRank, clustering, navigation endpoints.

roadmap.name: Legal chat
roadmap.desc: Five LLM providers, FastMCP tool server, conversation history.

roadmap.name: Dashboards
roadmap.desc: Compliance + analytics Plotly panels with interactive filters.

roadmap.name: React frontend
roadmap.desc: Layout, law explorer, graph canvas, chat UI, dashboards.

items.a: LexFlow is an open-source platform (Apache 2.0) that turns the full body of Spanish legislation from the BOE — around 12,000 laws in Markdown from the legalize-es project — into a navigable product with full-text search, a cross-reference graph, an AI chat that cites the real BOE, and legislative analytics dashboards. It runs locally as a single binary.
Yes, completely. LexFlow is free software under the Apache 2.0 license: you can use, modify and distribute it at no cost. There are no paid plans or paid features.

legalize-es is a public Git repository containing the full body of Spanish BOE legislation in Markdown, maintained by the community and updated regularly. LexFlow uses it as its data corpus: it parses those Markdown files to build the search index, the cross-reference graph and the chat context.

Yes. LexFlow is local-first: the law corpus is indexed on your machine at startup. If you use Ollama as your AI provider, the chat also works completely offline. With other providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) model queries go to the cloud, but BOE data never leaves your machine.

items.q: Which AI models does it support?
items.a: LexFlow supports five providers through MCP tools: Ollama (local, offline), LM Studio (local), OpenAI, Anthropic and Google. The active provider is switched from settings without restarting the app.

items.q: Which operating systems does it support?
items.a: LexFlow is available as a standalone binary for Windows, macOS and Linux. No Python, Docker or additional dependencies required.

authors.title: The people who build it.
authors.sub: LexFlow is the open-source work of two people. If the direction of the project interests you — or you'd like to contribute — their GitHub profiles are where the code lives.

list.name: Víctor
list.bio: Junior AI Engineer at NTT DATA Spain (A Coruña), working across many areas of AI, from computer vision research to research into LLM behavior, agentic AI solutions and tooling such as Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. BSc in Intelligent Systems Engineering from UIE, graduated with a 9.0/10 average and 4 Distinctions (Matrícula de Honor); thesis awarded Distinction (Matrícula de Honor) and recommended for conversion into a research paper. Now pursuing a Master's Degree in Artificial Intelligence Research (AEPIA / UIMP) alongside his full-time role. Building AI for Formula 1, from telemetry modelling to multi-agent reasoning. LexFlow's architect: designed and built the legalize-es parser, the NetworkX graph, the MCP integration and the React frontend.

list.name: Santiago
list.bio: Future engineer passionate about the aviation industry. Building intelligent systems with AI and data to enhance performance and reinforce safety. Co-designer of LexFlow: contributes to the backend, analytics dashboards and the quality of the legislative data corpus.

downloads.title: Download it for your platform.
downloads.subtitle: Desktop app is pre-alpha — no signed binaries yet, but upcoming releases will land here.
downloads.fallbackLink: Browse the Releases page on GitHub

ctaTitle: Spin it up in under a minute.
ctaSub: Clone the repo, run `uv sync`, and the full API is live at `localhost:8000` with interactive docs ready to go. If the project direction resonates, drop a star.
paraDevsTitle: If you want to peek under the hood.
paraDevsSub: Stack, internal architecture and the commands to run the project locally. Everything below is optional — the app works without touching any of it.

footer.tagline: Spanish legislation, alive and connected. An open-source platform built on legalize-es.
footer.legal: Apache 2.0 · pre-alpha · Built in the open · 2026
